title: daphne glabra
intro: Daphne glabra, also known as the Spurge Laurel, is a perennial shrub native to Asia and Europe, characterized by its glossy, dark green leaves and clusters of small, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Daphne glabra include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce anxiety and stress. Daphne glabra can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
